events.title: Paramshakti HPC Workshop
events.description: A hands-on workshop introducing researchers and students to Paramshakti HPC usage workflows.

events.title: Network Core Maintenance
events.description: Scheduled maintenance for core network devices with temporary interruption windows in selected buildings.

services.title: Internet Access
services.description: Captive portal authentication for wired and wireless internet access using ERP login and campus network password.
services.summary: Internet access in IIT Kharagpur uses web-based Captive Portal Authentication for both wired and wireless networks. Users authenticate with their ERP user ID and the campus network password set through ERP.

details.title: General Information
items: Internet access in IIT Kharagpur will use web-based authentication mechanism i.e. via Captive Portal Authentication. All users of both WiFi and wired network must finally connect to internet using a valid user id and password. At this time, users with ERP login are requested to authenticate to avail the institute Internet facility from both the wireless and wired networks.

details.title: Authentication Requirement
items: The campus network password is created in ERP and should be different from the ERP password.
This same campus network password is used for both internet access and Wi-Fi authentication.
You have to create this password only once unless you want to change it later. It can be changed anytime from ERP in the same manner.

details.title: Session Timeout
items: There is a 45 days session timeout present in the Captive Portal. You will have to repeat the authentication steps every 45 days on a particular device or machine to continue internet access.

instructions.title: To access the internet from wired network

items.text: Create your network access password in ERP.

children.text: Log in to your ERP account.
Click on the link "Set Campus Network Password" under menu item "Other Actions".
Set a password you will use for internet access. It should be different from your ERP password. This same password will also be used for WiFi Authentication.

items.text: Authenticate your device for internet access using the guide for your location.

children.text: Academic areas, offices, hostels, and guest houses.

references.label: Authentication Steps for Academic areas/Offices/Hostels/Guest houses

items.text: Connect to the internet using your ERP login as user id and the network password that you set in ERP. It may take 5-10 seconds for connection, please be patient.

instructions.title: To access the internet from wireless network

items.text: Connect your device to secured SSIDs for wireless network access.

children.text: Use the Wi-Fi Authentication manuals to configure your device.

items.text: Authenticate your device for internet access using the guide for your location.

children.text: Academic areas, offices, hostels, and guest houses.

references.label: Authentication Steps for Academic areas/Offices/Hostels/Guest houses

items.text: Connect to the internet using your ERP login as user id and the network password that you set in ERP. It may take 5-10 seconds for connection, please be patient.

documents.title: Official Internet Access Authentication Page
documents.description: Primary CIC page for internet access authentication workflow.

documents.title: Authentication Steps for Academic Areas, Offices, Hostels, and Guest Houses
documents.description: Official CIC PDF for regular internet access authentication.

documents.title: Authentication Steps for Residential Areas
documents.description: Official CIC PDF for residential-area internet access authentication.

documents.title: Wi-Fi Authentication Manuals
documents.description: Official CIC Wi-Fi Authentication page linked from the wireless internet access steps.

importantNotes: There will be no authentication or password required for accessing the IITKGP local resources like Zimbra mail, Apna, IITKGP websites etc. from wired network.

If user is already logged in, do not refresh the captive portal page or open the captive portal page again. It will be logged out and the user will need to login again to get the institute internet service.

New WiFi user need to authenticate two times to gain internet access. One for the first time WiFi access and second for the usual internet access. Second time onwards the WiFi user need to login only in captive portal to get the internet service.
There is no browser dependency for captive portal authentication but it is always recommended to use an updated browser for best user experience.
Faculty and staff may share their password with their family members for internet access. However, in order to avoid password misuse, there will be a limit for number of simultaneous connections.
The user with the user id used for logging in is solely responsible for any misuse of the internet when connected with that user id.
highPriorityNotes: Do not refresh or reopen the captive portal page while already logged in; it can log the user out.
The user id used for login is solely responsible for any misuse of internet access through that session.

contact.name: CIC Internet Access Support
contact.role: Primary contact persons for internet access authentication
contact.note: Please contact CIC to report problems or for any other queries. Primary contact persons are Mr. Debnath Pramanik (debnathp@cc.iitkgp.ac.in, Ph: 82378), Mr. Uddipan Tunga (uddipan@cc.iitkgp.ac.in, Ph: 83047), and Mr. Alokes Chattopadhyay (alokes@cc.iitkgp.ac.in, Ph: 82376).

services.title: Wi-Fi Authentication
services.description: 802.1x based wireless authentication for secured SSIDs using ERP login and campus network password.
services.summary: Wireless network access in IIT Kharagpur uses 802.1x based authentication. Users with ERP login should connect to the secured SSIDs using their ERP user ID and campus network password.

details.title: General Information
items: Wireless network access in IIT Kharagpur will use 802.1x based authentication. All users of the Wi-Fi network must finally connect using a valid user id and password. At this time, users with ERP login are requested to connect to the secured SSIDs for all wireless network access.

details.title: Authentication Requirement
items: The campus network password is created in ERP and should be different from the ERP password.
This same campus network password is also used for Internet Access Authentication.
You have to create this password only once unless you want to change it later. It can be changed anytime from ERP in the same manner.

items.text: Create your network access password in ERP.

children.text: Log in to your ERP account.
Click on the link "Set Campus Network Password" under menu item "Other Actions".
Set a password you will use for network access. It should be different from your ERP password. This same password will also be used for Internet Access Authentication.

items.text: Configure your device for Wi-Fi authentication using the relevant instruction guide.

references.label: Configuration Instruction for Windows Lumia based phones

items.text: You have to do this configuration only once on a device or machine per secured SSID unless you change your password or want to login with another user id.

Connect to the desired SSID using your ERP login as user id and the network password that you set in ERP. It may take 10-15 seconds for connection the first time you configure and connect, so please be patient. Normally the device will remember the user id and password and will automatically connect with this in future.

documents.title: Configuration Instruction for Android devices
documents.description: Configuration instruction for Android devices.

documents.title: Configuration Instruction for Windows 10 machines (pdf)
documents.description: Configuration instruction for Windows 10 machines.

documents.title: Configuration Instruction for Linux machines
documents.description: Configuration instruction for Linux machines.

documents.title: Configuration Instruction for Windows 7 machines
documents.description: Configuration instruction for Windows 7 machines.

documents.title: Configuration Instruction for Windows Lumia based phones
documents.description: Configuration instruction for Windows Lumia based phones.

documents.title: Configuration Instruction for Mac devices
documents.description: Configuration instruction for Mac devices.

importantNotes: Wi-Fi access uses the ERP user ID and campus network password; keep the password secure, and update saved device credentials whenever the password is changed.
Device configuration is usually required only once per secured SSID, unless the password changes or the device needs to connect with another user ID.
Faculty and staff may share access with family members, subject to simultaneous-connection limits, but the logged-in user ID remains solely responsible for all Wi-Fi activity and misuse.
highPriorityNotes: The user id used for Wi-Fi login is solely responsible for any misuse of the Wi-Fi network through that connection.

services.title: Mail Access
services.description: Institute-managed mail services for faculty, staff, and students through Zimbra and Google mail workflows.
services.summary: CIC centrally manages institute mail services using Zimbra for faculty, staff, and students enrolled before 2020-21, and G-Suite for students from the 2020-21 Autumn session onward.

details.title: General Information
items: The institute provides mail services to faculty, staff, and students using Zimbra, an enterprise class email, calendar, and collaboration solution, and G-Suite for students from the 2020-21 Autumn session onward.
The mail messaging facility is centrally managed by CIC.

details.title: Mail Platform Split
items: Faculty, staff, and students enrolled before 2020-21 use the institute Zimbra mail workflow.
Students enrolled from the 2020-21 Autumn session onward use the Google mail workflow.

items.text: Faculty, staff, and students enrolled before 2020-21 can access institute webmail through the Zimbra mail service.
Students enrolled from the 2020-21 Autumn session onward can access mail through Google Mail.
Outlook users can follow the setup and archiving references.

instructions.title: Third Party Mail Client Configuration
items: Use these settings to access mail services through third-party utilities, mail clients, or mobile devices from both inside and outside the IIT Kharagpur network.
Select the configuration table that matches the account category.

tables.title: Faculty, Staff, and Students Enrolled Before 2020-21
rows:
iitkgpmail.iitkgp.ac.in
Requires SSL: Yes
Requires TLS: Yes (if available)
Requires Authentication: Yes
Port for SSL: 465
Port for TLS/STARTTLS: 587

tables.title: Students Enrolled From 2020-21 Autumn Session Onward
rows:
smtp.gmail.com
Requires SSL: Yes
Requires TLS: Yes (if available)
Requires Authentication: Yes
Port for SSL: 465
Port for TLS/STARTTLS: 587

documents.title: Zimbra Webmail
documents.description: Web based mail access for faculty, staff, and students enrolled before 2020-21.

documents.title: Google Mail
documents.description: Web based mail access for students enrolled from the 2020-21 Autumn session onward.

documents.title: Outlook mail client setup
documents.description: Reference PDF for setting up Outlook as a mail client.

documents.title: Archiving emails using Outlook
documents.description: Reference PDF for archiving emails using Outlook.

importantNotes: Choose the correct mail workflow before configuring webmail or a third-party mail client.
Use SSL-enabled IMAP, POP, and SMTP settings when configuring third-party mail utilities or mobile clients.
Outgoing SMTP requires authentication, and TLS/STARTTLS should be used where available.

contact.name: CIC Mail Support
contact.role: Institute mail and client configuration support

services.title: Labs
services.description: Academic computing labs with Windows and Linux environments for classes, workshops, conferences, and institute activities.
services.summary: CIC operates institute software labs used for regular classes, placement activities, workshops, conferences, and other approved academic programs.

details.title: Lab Capacity
items: CIC manages more than 450 PCs in software labs.
Both Windows and Linux environments are available.
Labs are used for classes, workshops, conferences, and placement-related activities.

details.title: Usage Scope
items: The labs are intended primarily for academics and approved institute activities.
Commercial software is made available where required and where licensing permits.

instructions.title: How To Request Lab Use
items: Check that the requirement is aligned with classes, workshops, placements, conferences, or another approved institute purpose.
Complete the relevant lab booking form.
Route the request to CIC for approval.
Coordinate software, timing, and operational requirements with CIC after approval.

documents.title: Official Labs Page
documents.description: Primary CIC page for software labs and lab usage.

documents.title: Software Repository
documents.description: Repository site commonly referenced in the lab ecosystem.

importantNotes: Open-source tools are encouraged wherever possible.
Commercial software availability depends on institute licenses and academic needs.
Lab access is controlled through approval rather than direct self-service reservation.

services.title: Softwares
services.description: Central access to licensed software such as Matlab, Abaqus, SolidWorks, Ansys, SPSS, and Microsoft campus licenses.
services.summary: CIC manages a broad catalog of institute-licensed software used in teaching, research, administration, and campus operations.

children.text: For more information on the MATLAB Campus-Wide License, refer to the IIT Kharagpur MATLAB Portal.
For issues related to your MATLAB installation, contact MathWorks Support.
If the installation had already expired, you will be prompted to activate when attempting to launch MATLAB. Proceed by reactivating MATLAB using your MathWorks account credentials.

details.title: Service Model
items: Different software categories follow different access and activation models.
Some tools use campus-license workflows while others depend on centrally managed access.
Software usage is expected to remain within institutional licensing terms.

instructions.title: How To Proceed
items: Identify whether the required software is part of the CIC-supported catalog.
Check whether access requires a repository download, activation workflow, or centralized license use.
Follow the software-specific instruction path issued by CIC.

Software-specific deployment details may vary across departments and use cases.

services.title: VPN
services.description: Secure remote access workflow for approved users with FortiClient setup, credentials, and CIC-managed configuration.
services.summary: CIC provides VPN services to faculty for special requirements on a case-by-case basis. The service uses FortiClient and VPN credentials issued through CIC.

details.title: Service Scope
items: VPN access is provided to faculty for special requirements on a case-by-case basis.
A VPN username and password are required before the service can be used.
The VPN client should be installed and configured according to the operating system in use.

details.title: Prerequisites
items: Make sure that internet is working properly on your system.

items.text: Obtain a VPN username and password through the VPN access request process.
Download FortiClient from the Fortinet website.
Download FortiClient from IITKGP LAN when connected inside the campus network.
Download FortiClient from the IITKGP VPN website when outside IIT LAN.
children: Log in with your VPN username and password.
Download FortiClient as per your operating system.
Log out after the download is complete.

items.text: Windows installation guide
Macintosh installation guide

documents.title: Official VPN Page
documents.description: Primary CIC page for VPN request and installation guidance.

documents.title: VPN Request Form
documents.description: Official CIC PDF for VPN access requests.

documents.title: FortiClient Installation For Windows
documents.description: Official CIC PDF for Windows VPN setup.

documents.title: FortiClient Installation For Mac
documents.description: Official CIC PDF for Mac VPN setup.

importantNotes: VPN access is provided only after approval and credential issuance through CIC.
Use the VPN credentials only for the approved institutional requirement.
Keep the issued VPN username and password secure, and log out when the VPN session is no longer required.

services.title: NTP
services.description: Campus time synchronization service for institute systems using the centrally provided CIC network time server.
services.summary: CIC provides a campus time-synchronization service for systems that need a stable institute time source inside IIT Kharagpur.

details.title: Service Information
items: The NTP service is intended for use from within the IIT Kharagpur campus network.
The published network time server is ntp.iitkgp.ac.in.
The service is useful for servers, labs, and systems where accurate timestamps matter.

instructions.title: How To Use The Service
items: Keep the device within the intended campus network context.
Configure the time synchronization source as ntp.iitkgp.ac.in.
Verify that the host is synchronizing correctly after configuration.

documents.title: Official NTP Service Page
documents.description: Primary CIC page for the campus NTP service.

importantNotes: The service is especially relevant where time drift affects logs or scheduled tasks.
Use the official CIC NTP hostname exactly as published.
The NTP service is framed as a campus infrastructure utility rather than a public internet endpoint.

contact.name: CIC Network Team
contact.role: Infrastructure timing and network services support

services.title: Meghamala
services.description: IIT Kharagpur cloud service for research computing, offering on-demand virtual machines and persistent storage on request.
services.summary: Meghamala is the IITKGP Cloud, conceptualized to address the computational needs of the research community at IIT Kharagpur through on-demand compute and storage services.

details.title: Service Overview
items: Meghamala was conceptualized to address the computational needs of the research community at IIT Kharagpur.
The service provides cloud-based compute and storage options for approved academic and research workloads.
Users can provision a virtual machine on demand and use it as a desktop environment or to run research workloads.

items.text: VMs4U Compute Nodes
children: Provision a virtual machine on demand and use it as a desktop or run your workload on it.
IITKGP_MEGH_VM configuration: 16 VCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 60 GB ephemeral storage.
Guest operating system options include Ubuntu, Rocky and Windows.

items.text: Storage on the House
children: Persistent storage is provided on request.

items.text: Click here to request for a VM

instructions.title: Requesting Meghamala Access
items: Review the available VM configuration and operating system options.
Submit a VM request through the Meghamala request workflow.
Use the allocated VM and storage for the approved research or academic workload.

documents.title: Official Meghamala Services Page
documents.description: Primary Meghamala page describing cloud compute and storage services.

documents.title: Request a Meghamala VM
documents.description: Request workflow for provisioning a Meghamala virtual machine.

importantNotes: Meghamala is intended for computational needs of the IIT Kharagpur research community.
Persistent storage is provided on request rather than as a default allocation.
Additional services may be added over time as the platform evolves.

contact.name: Meghamala Cloud Support
contact.role: Private cloud access and service assistance
contact.note: For Meghamala queries or feedback, contact the Meghamala support email.
